cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage carefully to figure out the correct answer. The passage talks about how memory can play tricks on us, sometimes leading us to remember things that didn't happen or forget things that did happen. The author gives two examples to illustrate this. First example: - The author got up one morning and found the front door was unlocked all night. - Yet, the author "clearly remember[ed] locking it carefully the night before."
